title: 1 Profitable Stock on Our Watchlist and 2 We Question
content:
Not all profitable companies are built to last - some rely on outdated models or unsustainable advantages. Just because a business is in the green today doesn’t mean it will thrive tomorrow.

Profits are valuable, but they’re not everything. At StockStory, we help you identify the companies that have real staying power. That said, here is one profitable company that balances growth and profitability and two that may struggle to keep up.

Two Stocks to Sell:

RingCentral (RNG)

Trailing 12-Month GAAP Operating Margin: 4.8%

Built on its proprietary Message Video Phone (MVP) platform that unifies multiple communication methods, RingCentral (NYSE:RNG) provides AI-driven cloud communications and collaboration solutions that enable businesses to connect through voice, video, messaging, and contact center services.

Why Do We Pass on RNG?

Offerings struggled to generate meaningful interest as its average billings growth of 3.8% over the last year did not impress Estimated sales growth of 4.5% for the next 12 months implies demand will slow from its two-year trend Customer acquisition costs take a while to recoup, making it difficult to justify sales and marketing investments that could increase revenue

RingCentral’s stock price of $37.62 implies a valuation ratio of 1.3x forward price-to-sales. To fully understand why you should be careful with RNG, check out our full research report (it’s free).

Nature's Sunshine (NATR)

Trailing 12-Month GAAP Operating Margin: 5.2%

Started on a kitchen table in Utah, Nature’s Sunshine (NASDAQ:NATR) manufactures and sells nutritional and personal care products.

Why Is NATR Not Exciting?

4.4% annual revenue growth over the last three years was slower than its consumer staples peers Modest revenue base of $480.1 million gives it less fixed cost leverage and fewer distribution channels than larger companies Subpar operating margin of 4.8% constrains its ability to invest in process improvements or effectively respond to new competitive threats

Nature's Sunshine is trading at $24.44 per share, or 23.4x forward P/E. Read our free research report to see why you should think twice about including NATR in your portfolio, it’s free.

One Stock to Watch:

Booking (BKNG)

Trailing 12-Month GAAP Operating Margin: 32.8%

Formerly known as The Priceline Group, Booking Holdings (NASDAQ:BKNG) is the world’s largest online travel agency.

Why Are We Positive On BKNG?

Prominent and differentiated platform leads to a best-in-class gross margin of 86.7% Share repurchases over the last three years enabled its annual earnings per share growth of 31.4% to outpace its revenue gains BKNG is a free cash flow machine with the flexibility to invest in growth initiatives or return capital to shareholders

Story Continues

At $4,368 per share, Booking trades at 12.5x forward EV/EBITDA. Is now the right time to buy? Find out in our full research report, it’s free.

High-Quality Stocks for All Market Conditions

WHILE YOU’RE HERE: Top 9 Market-Beating Stocks. The best stocks don't just beat the market once. They do it again. And again. Robust revenue growth, rising free cash flow, returns on capital that leave their competition in the dust. The market has already rewarded these businesses.

But our AI platform says the party isn't over. Find out which 9 stocks made the cut this week — FREE.  Get Our Top 9 Market-Beating Stocks for Free HERE.

title: How Charter’s AI Contact Center Push With RingCentral Shapes Its Broader Digital Strategy At CHTR
content:
Earlier this month, Charter Communications launched a Multiview feature on the Spectrum TV App, allowing customers with eligible packages to watch up to four NCAA basketball games simultaneously across devices including Xumo, Apple TV, Roku, Xbox, and major smart TV brands. Charter also expanded its partnership with RingCentral to bring AI-powered RingCX contact center and AI Conversation Expert tools to Spectrum Business customers, integrating communications, analytics, and customer engagement into a single cloud platform. We’ll now examine how Charter’s expanded AI-powered business offering with RingCentral may influence its broader investment narrative and outlook.

The best AI stocks today may lie beyond giants like Nvidia and Microsoft. Find the next big opportunity with these 22 smaller AI-focused companies with strong growth potential through early-stage innovation in machine learning, automation, and data intelligence that could fund your retirement.

Charter Communications Investment Narrative Recap

To own Charter today, you need to believe its core broadband and mobile bundles can remain attractive despite subscriber pressures and heavy debt. Near term, the key catalyst is whether Spectrum Mobile growth and network upgrades can offset broadband softness, while the biggest risk remains intense competition from fiber and fixed wireless. The new Multiview and AI contact center features are incremental product enhancements, but they do not materially change those primary drivers in the short run.

The expanded UCX with RingCentral offering stands out here, because it directly ties Charter’s AI investments to its business services segment. By layering RingCX and AI Conversation Expert on top of Spectrum’s managed network, Charter is trying to deepen its role with mid-market and enterprise customers, which aligns most closely with the existing catalyst around AI and service efficiency, even if the financial impact will take time to show up.

But beneath these product wins, investors should also be aware of rising competitive pressure in broadband and...

Read the full narrative on Charter Communications (it's free!)

Charter Communications' narrative projects $56.8 billion revenue and $6.0 billion earnings by 2028. This implies revenues declining by 0.9% per year and an earnings increase of about $0.7 billion from $5.3 billion today.

Uncover how Charter Communications' forecasts yield a $276.80 fair value, a 31% upside to its current price.

Exploring Other PerspectivesCHTR 1-Year Stock Price Chart

Some of the most optimistic analysts were already assuming Charter could reach about US$58.1 billion in revenue and US$7.0 billion in earnings by 2028, which is far more upbeat than the consensus view. When you compare that to the risk of heavier capital needs and competition highlighted above, you can see how widely opinions differ and why this latest AI and Multiview news could eventually shift those narratives in different directions.

Story Continues

Explore 6 other fair value estimates on Charter Communications - why the stock might be worth over 3x more than the current price!

The Verdict Is Yours

Disagree with existing narratives? Extraordinary investment returns rarely come from following the herd, so go with your instincts.

title: Charter Communications (CHTR) Expands Partnership With RingCentral
content:
Charter Communications, Inc. (NASDAQ:CHTR) is one of the Most Undervalued Long Term Stocks to Buy According to Analysts. On March 11, Charter Communications, Inc. (NASDAQ:CHTR) announced expanding its partnership with RingCentral to offer Spectrum Business customers UCX, a unified platform.

​UCX brings together RingCentral’s AI-powered tools for communications, contact centers, and analytics to the Charter Communications’ network. The partnership brings RingCX, which is an AI-driven contact center that can handle voice, video, SMS, web chat, social media, and more. Moreover, an AI Conversation Expert will also be added for Spectrum Business customers, which provides various services, including call transcriptions, spotting sales risks, and synchronizing insights to CRMs automatically.

​Management noted that the combination brings end-to-end visibility from customer contact to sales. It enables faster resolutions, better decisions, and growth. Moreover, the solution is tailored for regulated sectors including healthcare, education, government, and financial services.

​Both companies are set to showcase UCX with RingCentral at HIMSS 2026 and Enterprise Connect in Las Vegas.

Charter Communications, Inc. (NASDAQ:CHTR) is an American broadband connectivity company and cable operator that provides internet, video, mobile, and voice services.

While we acknowledge the potential of CHTR as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If you're looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also stands to benefit significantly from Trump-era tariffs and the onshoring trend, see our free report on the best short-term AI stock.

title: Enterprise Connect Debuts in Las Vegas with Record Energy, Sets Return for 2027
content:
Industry Leaders Zoom, AWS and RingCentral Unveil Major AI and CX Announcements

LAS VEGAS, March 13, 2026--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Enterprise Connect, the leading conference for enterprise communications and customer experience, brought together thousands of business leaders, decision-makers and industry titans for its inaugural Las Vegas show at Caesars Forum. Refreshed and modernized in its new location, 160 speakers, more than 90 exhibitors and over 2,700 total registrants helped Enterprise Connect successfully debut its West Coast show, which charts the course for a return to Las Vegas March 23-25, 2027 at the MGM Grand.

Attendees explored the new venue with a dynamic three-day program featuring powerful keynote speakers, an expansive Expo Hall and more than 80 sessions across five progressive conference tracks taking deep dives into AI, AV/video, CX/contact centers, collaboration and unified communications and security, compliance and management.

"Moving Enterprise Connect marked a pivotal moment for the show and the Communications and CX communities we serve," said Kara Shukas, Director, Events, Enterprise Connect. "Our Vegas debut drew more than 2,700 registrants, including 68 percent first-time participants and significantly stronger western region representation. We’re excited to build on that momentum when we return here in 2027."

Enterprise Connect Event Highlights

Keynotes Emceed by Justin Robbins, founder and principal analyst of Metric Sherpa, the Keynote Stage provided a platform for major announcements from Enterprise Connect partners. Zoom introduced new workflow orchestration capabilities across Zoom Workplace, Zoom Phone, and Zoom CX; RingCentral debuted its new agentic consumer engagement tool, AirPro and AirPro Healthcare; Amazon Web Services explored the customer experience paradox and the business need to deliver higher quality, lower costs, all at greater scale; and Dialpad outlined its emerging agentic platform designed to empower enterprises.

As the featured keynote speaker, Futurist Heather E. McGowan offered a forward-looking perspective on how these rapid advancements in AI, automation and collaboration technologies are reshaping the future of work and human interaction across the enterprise.

Exclusive Conference Summits Enterprise Connect’s exclusive summit series delivered focused, half-day deep dives into three of the most critical areas shaping enterprise communications today: AI, customer experience, and collaboration technology. The AI Acceleration—From Pilot to Pervasive Summit explored how organizations can move beyond experimentation to achieve real ROI and scale AI across the enterprise. Breakthrough Strategies for Your CX Revolution examined how AI, cloud migration, and intelligent automation are transforming modern contact centers and customer engagement. Meanwhile, Sight, Sound, Success: Mastering Collaboration/AV in the Evolving Workplace focused on modernizing collaboration infrastructure, optimizing meeting experiences, and supporting the demands of hybrid work environments.

Story Continues

Expo Hall Featuring more than 90 sponsors and exhibitors, the industry’s leading solution providers debuted their latest innovations in enterprise communications, collaboration, and customer experience. Attendees explored hands-on demonstrations from AWS, Dialpad, RingCentral, Zoom, Five9, Genesys, Infobip, Miratech, NiCE, Salesforce, Spectrum,Vonage and more, all highlighting AI-powered communications solutions, next-generation contact center solutions, unified communications tools, and hybrid-work collaboration technologies designed to modernize enterprise infrastructure.

Best of Enterprise Connect Awards Program

Best of Enterprise Connect Overall Winner: Crescendo Multimodal AI Best Innovation in Customer Experience: Crescendo Multomodal AI & NiCE CXOne Most Innovative Use of AI: Rime Arcana V2 Best Innovation for AV/Video: Cisco Workspace Designer Best Innovation for Security/Compliance: Intrado OneAlert & Vonage Agentforce Identity Insights

IT Hero Awards

Tabitha Almanza, Director, Procurement, YETI Nathaniel Beck, IT Manager, Johnson Controls International Andrew Benson, Chief Innovation Officer, Envision Radiology Jose F. Hernandez, Communication Systems Engineer, Brigham Young University Mattias Liljeberg, Manager, IT Telecom, Louisiana BLUE

Women in Communications Spotlight Award Winners

Monica Deal, Sr. Vice President, Global Customer Operations, Lexis Nexis Lisa Diehl, Director, Customer Care, Freshpet Beth Linthicum, Executive Director, Call Quality, Analytics and CX, Aetna/CVS Health Millicent McIntyre, Senior Director, Buyer Services, IAA (Insurance Auto Auctions) Patrycja Sobera, SVP & GM for Digital Workplace Solutions, Unisys

For more information on the news and announcements from Enterprise Connect 2026, visit www.enterpriseconnect.com.

Stay connected with Enterprise Connect AI on X, LinkedIn, YouTube, Facebook and Instagram.

About Enterprise Connect For over 35 years, Enterprise Connect has stood at the forefront of enterprise communications, collaboration, and customer experience in North America. Delivering an impactful blend of industry-best conference tracks, innovative networking experiences, dynamic expo activations, and year-round digital engagement, Enterprise Connect also provides the very best in webinar hostings, virtual events and newsletters. Bringing together IT executives, vendors, analysts, or channel partners, attendees engage with influential peers and critical innovations across themes like AI-powered systems, CX platforms, cloud migration, and modern workplace strategies. Enterprise Connect is organized by Informa, a leading international events, digital services and academic research group. Informa is a FTSE 100 company, with 14,000 colleagues working in 30 countries. www.enterpriseconnect.com
